user_prompt: implement `void test_nvsloadsave()`, start the serial port, and load up BtHostList and WifiManager from NVS, read all the data items to Serial port, then write a the maximum amount of data (use the alphabet to fill up the strings) and then save them
I need to know if the NVS works or if we've exceeded some sort of size limit or need some compile time partitioning trickery
agent_reply: - Prints NVS stats before load, after save, and after verify.
- Dumps the full fixed-size tables to Serial, including passwords because this is explicitly a diagnostic test.
- Fills max-sized strings with repeated alphabet data.
- Writes max-size `network_cfg_t` and `bt_host_list_t` data to NVS.
- Reloads both managers and dumps the verified result.

One note: `WifiManager` still applies current security-level policy during sanitize, so counts like AP/cloud allowed entries may clamp, but the fixed arrays are filled before the full struct blob is saved.

Verification: I ran `pio run -e thefly_sec1`. The new test file compiled; the build failed later at link with the existing `.iram0.text` overflow by `2104 bytes`, not from this code. The build also regenerated [web_assets.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/web_assets.h) because the package script ran.
